specification.topic-statement: The VCR HTTP layer intercepts HTTP client operations during test runs to deliver pre-recorded response fixtures instead of making live network requests.

scope.in-scope: VCR state tracking per HTTP handle, write-callback interception for record and playback modes, chunk delivery during playback, synthetic completion signaling, HTTP status code injection, and passthrough behavior when VCR is inactive.
scope.boundary: This layer wraps each HTTP client operation. When VCR is inactive, every operation is forwarded unchanged to the underlying library. When VCR is active, operations are intercepted as described.

This layer queries the fixture system for the active VCR mode, retrieves response status codes and chunks during playback, records captured chunks during recording, and checks whether more data remains. The fixture system itself is outside scope.

field.name: handle
field.note: the HTTP easy handle this state belongs to

field.name: http status
field.note: HTTP response status code from the fixture

behavior.inactive-mode: When VCR is not active, all HTTP client calls are forwarded directly to the underlying library with their arguments unchanged. No state tracking occurs.
behavior.write-callback-interception:
When the caller registers a write callback on an HTTP handle, the VCR layer intercepts this registration.
In record mode, the caller's callback and context are saved in per-handle state. A wrapper callback is registered with the library in place of the caller's callback, and the per-handle state is registered as the context. When data arrives, the wrapper first forwards the chunk to the fixture system for recording, then calls the original callback with the original context. If the original callback is absent, the wrapper consumes the data silently and reports success.
In playback mode, the caller's callback and context are saved in per-handle state. The registration call returns success without forwarding anything to the underlying library — no callback is registered with it.
behavior.handle-lifecycle:
When an HTTP handle is added to a multi-handle in playback mode, the HTTP response status code is retrieved from the fixture system and stored in the per-handle state. The message-delivered flag is reset to false for the new request. The underlying library call is still made.
When an HTTP handle is cleaned up, any per-handle VCR state for that handle is removed before the underlying cleanup call is made.
behavior.playback-data-delivery:
When the HTTP multi-handle is polled for progress in playback mode, the next chunk is retrieved from the fixture system. If a chunk is available, it is delivered to the write callback of the first handle in the state list that has a registered callback. The running-handles count is set to 1 if more chunks remain, or 0 if the fixture is exhausted. If no chunk is available, running-handles is set to 0. Success is always returned.
Chunk delivery targets only the first handle in the state list that has a registered callback. When multiple HTTP handles are active within one multi-handle, chunks for subsequent handles are not delivered. The implementation assumes at most one active handle per multi-handle.
behavior.playback-wait: When the HTTP multi-handle wait operation is called in playback mode, it returns immediately with success. The number-of-file-descriptors-ready output is set to 1 to simulate activity. No actual waiting occurs.
behavior.playback-completion-signaling:
When the message-read operation is called in playback mode:
If the message has already been delivered for this request, zero messages are reported and nothing is returned.
If the fixture has no more chunks and at least one handle exists in the state list, a completion message is synthesized. The message carries the type "done", the handle from the first entry in the state list, and a success result code. The delivered flag is set to true. Zero further messages are reported. The synthesized message is returned.
If the fixture still has chunks remaining, zero messages are reported and nothing is returned.
The synthesized completion message is written into a single static object. Concurrent or overlapping requests would overwrite this object.
behavior.playback-status-retrieval: When the caller queries the HTTP response code for a handle in playback mode, the status code stored in the per-handle state (populated when the handle was added to the multi-handle) is written to the output parameter and success is returned. All other info queries are forwarded to the underlying library.

compile-time-scope.notable: This entire interception layer is only compiled in debug and test builds. In release builds, the HTTP wrappers are defined as direct passthrough functions inlined at the call site and the VCR layer does not exist.
